62471032

>>62470662
Rockets launched from Wenchang fall into the sea, not onto populated areas. It's the rockets launched from Xichang that fall onto populated areas. This is because the location for Xichang spaceport was chosen during the Cold War when security from military strike was considered very important. There are no unpopulated in the Chinese interior at the low latitudes from which you can launch to geosynchronous orbit. Xichang spaceport will probably be closed within 5 years, or at most 10 years.

If you think those are the same thing I can see why you are frustrated with someone pointing out the error to begin with.

The market decides the price. Miners have 2 options:

1. Sell now to cover costs
2. Eat the costs and wait for the market to improve

Neither scenario is them setting the price. Scenario 2 is miners speculating with coins the same way any market participant does, except their tokens were previously uncirculating.

item.title: Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK? — 66 replies — 264.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK?

Open thread • Replies: 66 • Updated: 2026-07-15 16:43 UTC

OP

Every time I see the UK mentioned here, it is always in a negative light.

It is always
>In the USA, wages are better!
>In Europe, houses are cheaper!
>The FTSE is underperforming!
>My taxes are too high!

There is never any mention of the fantastic benefits which come with life in the UK. The fantastic healthcare for minimal cost, the great education system available to everybody, the dynamic modern economy with many bustling sectors, and everything else.

Taxes are higher than the USA but they are lower than most of Europe and we still enjoy fantastic public services whereas in America there are no public services or safety nets.

Not to mention the things you cannot measure. Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs. Enjoying the fantastic beautiful nature and gardens. Living in the historical majesty and balancing that with modern cosmopolitan qualities.

The UK might not be the best place to become rich but nobody is poor here and it is very easy to enjoy life if you have goals beyond the number in your bank account.

Early replies (first 3)

62481973
>>62481958
the problem is that its being filled with immigrants who are living off tax payers money
thats the gist of why everyone is so pissed off here

62481986
>>62481973
So just right wing racebaiting?

No, I think it runs deeper than that. Most people have not personally had negative experiences with migrants, and reading the Daily Mail does not count.

I am trying to angle from a financial perspective too. People talk about the UK like it is a poor country but it is one of the richest countries on the planet. I think perhaps they are just spoiled and do not know what poor countries are really like. I blame social media.

62481994
>>62481958
>fantastic healthcare for minimal cost
my nan lived in birmingham and she came down with leukaemia and was gone within a couple of months. She never even got to see a doctor because the muslim immigrants were getting seen first. if i had something to lose i wouldnt even dare post this for fear of legal reprimand.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62483307

it's a woke version of north korea that's rapidly turning into the third world. in a few decades, actual brits living there will be a persecuted minority, like in south africa, you stupid re-dditor.

62483347
>>62483304
I often wonder what it must be like, seething about niggers and kikes 24/7, spending your every waking moment obsessing over white genocide and BLACKED porn... What a miserable existence it must be.

62483373
Mockery coming from Americans and Canadians are justified because those countries still offer much better living standards for middle and working class people and a higher level of personal liberties, although Canada is obviously on a downward trend.

Europeans (except for Switzerland and Norway) can't say shit about us. I don't care what cope statistics they pull out of their arses. Eurostat is fake and gay. I've lived in Europe and Britain is a capitalist paradise compared to them. That's why in the days of free movement it was Eurofaggots moving here and not the other way around. They get taxed to shit. Most EU countries don't have a tax free personal allowance. Most of them don't even have anything similar to a stock and shares ISA. A shocking number of them have CGT on unrealised gains. Europoors can't even withdraw a tax free lump sum from their retirement pots and they all have regulations forcing providers to invest in local businesses so when a French pays into his private pension pot which by the way, most people don't even have one since they have very little left to invest after their insane income tax levels, he won't be getting S&P500 level returns, his money will go straight into Louis Vuitton stocks or something gay like that.

They will talk shit about our economy's over reliance on the financial sector while ignoring the fact a dozen European countries still rely on tourism. Fucking tourism, like some Caribbean shithole.

And the way their "common market" works leads to some pretty wild and grossly inaccurate numbers that inflate their income levels. Every book sold by Amazon in the EU is officially sold by Luxembourg. Most tech companies are based in Ireland which gives them a hilarious $140k GDP per capita. Anyone who's been to Dublin can tell they're much poorer than that. Their wealthiest city looks like a gentrified Sheffield and we're supposed to believe they're the 3rd wealthiest people on the planet.

62483377
UK was cool 2500 years ago.

62483416
>>62483373
Look! He's trying to make out that England isn't a sick, cringeworthy country for the mentally retarded!

62483981
>>62481958
UK used to be a S tier zone, now it's more like an A- or a B+ zone. I'm from Eastern Europe and if I moved to UK it 100% wouldn't be worth it economically, would most likely be a downgrade in living conditions.
So Eastern Europe living conditions with mass migrants on top.

I'm a retard on this topic but my guess is that there has been fuck all investments in the UK since 1980s, because the big capital had a preference for asian and african regions for both ideological and profit reasons. No investments + exclusively extractory business practices for ~40 years will bring such results. So economically UK is like an old, neglected region that stagnated so hard that even Eastern Europe (that was rekt by 50 years of communism) is becoming a better place to live. Certainly a much better place to settle down and start a family.
Culturally it's a sad joke, a parody of its former self.

It's not even worth it to visit UK for a few years to accumulate savings, because it offers you no such prospects. UK has literally nothing to offer to Europeans. And most bongs would be better off somewhere else. It's in a very weird position right now, and degrading rapidly.

62484019

62484139

>>62483373
Quick comparison.

UK:
In my sector I should be able to land a 90 000 £ / year job in the UK. That's about 5200 £ per month after taxes. For my family the living expenses (rent, bills, council tax, food, everyday expenses) would total to about ~4200 £ a month, so would be able to live reasonably comfortably and save 1000 £ a month.

Eastern Europe:
In Eastern Europe I can land a 5000 £ / month job, 4000 £ after taxes. Same expenses around here would cost around 2000 £ a month. So I can save twice as much as in the UK.

Now let's compare it after 5, 10 and 15 years of savings with +8% per year.

UK: 5/10/15 - 73k / 182 / 346 £
EE: 5/10/15 - 146k / 365k / 692k £

UK is a huge downgrade from Eastern Europe. And that's regardless of the old, failing infrastructure and mass migration.

Companies managing billions of dollars aren’t going to want to invest in new tech because, again, there are no clear rules. If the act passes then more institutions will feel comfortable using blockchain technology.

item.title: Online Money Making? — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Online Money Making?

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-07-15 16:32 UTC

OP

I desperately need a way to make money. Don't care if it's a job, legal, illegal, whatever, it just needs to be a thing I can do from my computer.

I'm disabled - I can't stand for more than 20 minutes without passing out, and can't lift anything more than 10 lbs. Doing the dishes takes me over an hour because I have to take breaks. Applied for disability but it'll be a while before I start getting anything from there and I need food now.

It HAS to be something I can "set my own hours" for to let me take breaks and get my chores done. It has to be 100% doable from a computer. I do not have any work experience or formal education. I've become disheartened trying to compete for jeet pay and sending in thousands of applications online after sifting through all the "remote" jobs that actually need you to come in to a physical building sometimes or require a base minimum of a college degree and like 10 years working in the field. The fact that I can smell the pizza my neighbors ordered but I'm so fucking poor I can't buy one too is making me consider the rope. I'm so hungry for something hot and tasty but all I have is peanut butter sandwiches. I will do just about anything that doesn't involve porn or sex.

I'm not even asking for much. Obviously more money is better, but if I can just make like $500 a week I'd be doing way better.

Early replies (first 3)

62479087
>>62479075
I think if you can make $500 per week you're going to get disqualified for disability. Or wind up in prison for fraud.

but what I do is flip stuff on ebay. it's easier if you can go to thrift stores and garage sales, but it can also be done entirely online. buy cheap auctions on ebay and then sell them as buy it now for twice as much or more. But to do that you need money to buy stuff, time to study prices, a place to store stuff to sell, a ton of shipping materials, and then months or years for things to sell.

but if you get into it and list enough stuff you can pretty easily get to the point where you're making thousands per month. It just takes time and money.

62479115
>>62479087
You can make like $2,800 a month before disability starts getting mad at you. If you make more than that they'll just lower the benefits you get, unless it's obvious that you're not actually disabled. I know a woman who faked a disability claim and then made Facebook posts of her on vacation and on hikes and shit and yeah they got mad enough to charge her with fraud. That won't be an issue for me. Theoretically if I find something I can do easily enough and if it's really profitable I can avoid getting disability payments altogether. I don't like being on charity.

I've tried selling random stuff on ebay and it just seems like it's so slow, nobody wants to buy anything or if they do they want to argue to hell and back over the price. Thank you, though.

62482161
>>62479075
take some money and go on telegram. find a guy that sells good leads. then text all of them a fake bank fraud alert about suspicious activity on their account and they need to type 1 to connect with an agent. then call all of those people pretending to be from the bank and talk them out of their savings

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62482172
>>62479087
What is the best type of product to look at to maximize your earning as an ebay flipper. Clothes, toys, videogames?

I go to japan once a year for work and can buy a bunch of exclusive shit here in person.

62482194
>>62479115
>$2,800 a month on disability
>Meanwhile I bust my ass 40 hours a week for $3100/month + meager benefits + a chance at extra money if I decide to work extra for the overtime slog
How do I get on disability? I need more free time..

62482199
>>62482172
You’re a loser

62482205
>>62482199
I looked this term up and it doesn't seem to be a category of product

62482221
>>62482194
By being fucking disabled. By the way, you get $1,600 from the government per month, it's only if you work and make $2,800 or more at a job that they start reducing or eliminating your benefits, you're not GETTING $2,800. If you want $1,600 per month at the cost of no vacation, active free time or hobbies, I guess do a backflip off a building to paralyze yourself. Apparently social security is going to run out in less than a decade, so people like me who can't fucking work or live are screwed anyway no matter what we do. Good thing my disease is fatal in less than that, I won't have to figure it out. For right now I just want to enjoy shit like hot meals and maybe leave something for my family, or at least cover funeral costs.

>>62482172
>>62482205
Look up one of those "import straight from Japan" webstores and see what sorts of products are popular. It'll also give you an idea of how much you can mark up the products. If I had to hazard a guess, probably shit like anime figurines and exclsuive snacks would be the most profitable. Weebs will pay anything for plastic anime titty statues and most people would at least try an exotic snack if you offered.

62482261
>>62482221
>you get $1,600 from the government per month
My monthly expenses are $1300/month (rent+food+insurance+gas+etc) so that sounds perfect
>Hot meal
Learn to cook. Cooking in general is cheaper and a dollar saved is worth more than a dollar earned (because of taxes). If you want pizza, they often go for around $4 for a large pizza in my local grocer
[spoiler]I don't know what exactly your condition is but if you live in the US and near a city, look up blood plasma donation centers like CSL/Octaphrama/Biolife/etc. They will will pay you for your blood plasma, and when I used to go it amounted to around $120/week (over two weekly visits) and they often have new donor bonuses that amount to around $500 over two weeks. This is plasma donation and not whole blood donationso it can be done twice a week for payment. There are restrictions like needing a certain level of blood protein/iron levels and other health stats like blood pressure and pulse but I'd check it out regardless.[/spoiler]

62482320
>>62482261
I'm on disability as well (£1,500, whatever that is in burger money) and it kind of sucks. For starters it took forever to get to this point. Constant assessments and years spent living on half this amount. Half of it goes to rent anyway due to HCOL. As that guy said you are constantly threatened with being kicked off the program. The result of this is that you become too afraid to do anything at all. Even volunteer or take a course because they'll say you aren't disabled any more. It's very boring. At least you have your wagie job to keep you occupied. Idk about America but savings are severely restricted so it's not like I can have any fun money.

62482328
>>62482261
I love to cook and I'm very good at it, I cook almost all my meals. The problem is there are days when I just can't. I meal prep when I can so that I can just throw something in the microwave or oven, but my ability to prep ahead is really limited. If I have a string of extra bad days together then I'm left with only shit I can throw together in 5 mintues, no time to prepare anything hot from scratch. I also can't donate blood or plasma because my condition is not stable enough. I know how to drive but I'm not legally allowed to, so unless I get chaperoned or want to brave public transit I am stuck in my home. I have to get people to shop for me or use a service like Walmart+ to have it delivered for cheap. If I get a trip to the store I have to use one of those electric cart things, but since I look young and healthy on the outside I get people judging me because they think I'm lazy. I've had people confront me over it because "there are people who really need to use those". Yeah bitch, me. I even get people who inspect the car I'm in after parking in a disabled spot "to make sure" I have the placard.

If you want to get on disability, then be disabled. Otherwise you're stealing it from people like me who need it to live. There are also a lot of retards who steal it when they don't need to, and I wish everyone who does would die. I'd work if I was physically able to, I hate being a burden to people and I love doing things. I wish I could go out for walks again or ride horses or be able to take a trip to the coast in my own car whenever I wanted, and I'd give up just about anything to be able to. Don't take your ability to work and live under your own power for granted, anon.

62482336
>>62482172
not sure, I sell coins and collectibles because the price is highly subjective, they're fairly small and easy to ship, and there's tons of stuff for sale on eBay to pick from and flip.

I used to sell antique postcards but that's not nearly as hot as it was a couple years back. If I was importing stuff to sell I'd probably try to get toys and collectibles. Maybe video games. Most of that stuff can probably be bought directly from Japan now though. So I'm not sure. My expertise is in local collectibles. Antiques mostly. Bottles and papers and coins and junk from the 1800's.

62482365
>>62482328
Only two things have ever gotten me to cook consistently. 1) Having a bike, fuck dragging groceries home on the bus 2) Buying expensive cuts of meat/fish because they're easier to prepare. Lamb chops/salmon doesn't 50 different sauces and seasonings and hours of stewing.
I've never meal prepped, microwaved leftovers tastes like ass.

62482377
>>62482336
I've occasionally tried to sell my old stuff on ebay just to get rid of it and I noticed that video games and RC parts went fast. Bicycles and my handmade soap did not.

62482401
>>62482377
yes video games sell in days. I just don't make much profit from them because everyone knows what they're worth so it's hard to get them half price. Also there's tons of other sellers so you have to go cheap or you won't sell.

I try to buy stuff with no real set value so I can double or triple the price and someone will still come along and buy it anyways. Antique books, old art prints, ex libris bookplates, greeting cards, collectible cards, advertising. Shit like that has no real set value so if you know what people want you can buy it cheap and sell it high

62482464
>>62479075
Suck cock online

62482528
>>62479075
Pays $14/hour or minimum wage if it's higher. 20 hours a week usually, but sometimes up to 35. Set your own hours. You have to study for an exam unpaid and pass to get hired.
https://jobs.telusdigital.com/jobs/17318859-us-raters

62482606
>>62482320
That's about $2,000, a little over. It does take a while to get on disability here too but I'm fast tracked because of my disease so hopefully I can get it sooner.

>>62482365
Meal prepping is, like, the #1 most important part of cooking, at least if you have limited physical ability. Whenever you have a minute or two you just cut up some more veggies into a tupperware or measure out some seasoning or line up the ingredients you're going to use for your next meal. It doesn't sound like a lot, but it means the difference between doing 30 minutes prep + 20 minutes cook time at once or like 5 minutes prep and 20 minutes cook time at once. You can also meal prep just about anything. Slow cooker, one pot, or quick prep recipes are great, you can find a lot of cookbooks dedicated to that sort of stuff. I also think a rice cooker is really important, if you're having a bad day you just take a few minutes to put the rice on and throw some fish or chicken in the oven for a quick dinner. Get one of the rice cookers with a steamer piece so you can also steam some veggies. The main thing that gets me is you can't really meal prep like a week ahead if you're using meat, but I need to eat meat heavy (preferably keto but that hasn't been working out), so if I fall behind because of a few bad days then I'm stuck with whatever pantry staples I can slap together in less than 5 minutes. I do keep frozen veggie steamer bags and frozen meals for cases like this, but they're honestly so gross that I only use them if I'm out of other options. What I need is a fucking live-in maid or butler so I can point to shit and have it done for me when I can barely get out of bed.

>>62482528
Oh shit, great find! I had no idea this was a thing. I'll send in an application. Any recs for what a company like this would want to hear? Work experience, my hobbies, etc. I'm not very knowledgeable about AI or techy stuff.

62482821
>>62482606
>Any recs for what a company like this would want to hear?
They want people with a high school diploma/GED and awareness of US culture. That's pretty much it.

There's also this position, which is what I do. It's considered easier I think, but I'm not certain they're hiring for it since it's not on the main website. But I found the job listing elsewhere:
https://www.ziprecruiter.com/jobs/telus-international/internet-safety-evaluator-english-us?lvk=D8IiHgM8tg1BDuMuVrzfJw

A tip for the exam: If you fail, there's usually a second attempt. Use OBS to record your first attempt in case you fail so you can figure out what you answered wrong.

62482922
>>62482821
Thank you! It seems like they'll keep your information on file and let you know when there are new job openings even if you don't get hired, so if I don't get the rater job I might get another one offered.

62484461
>>62482922
fuck a job bro. go to onlyscams.org

62484510
>>62479115
>I've tried selling random stuff on ebay and it just seems like it's so slow
anon, you can't just find random objects around your house and list a couple and expect them to sell. You have to find the items and categories with the most sales then hone in on specific items to search for and bid on. The trick is knowing what things have already sold for, how often they generally sell, how fast they sell, and whether or not you can bid snipe the same product for 50% or less of what you can resell it fast for. Its just a little research, adding things to a spreadsheet and then you're off to finding stuff to bid on. Once you have a catalog of items you'll just have sales like a normal store. Some stuff will sit in your inventory for a while but if the margin on sale is high enough it won't really concern you if you have other stuff selling. People often sell laptops with dead batteries for way less than it would cost you to buy another battery, open up the case, and install it because people are scared of opening up laptop cases.

She wasn't even really disabled, she claimed depression but she was depressed because all she did was sit around the house all day, every day smoking cigarettes, taking naps, and watching tv.

item.title: Why is there so much negativity surrounding the DRK? — 11 replies — 11.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is there so much negativity surrounding the DRK?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-15 15:45 UTC

OP

Every time I see the DRK mentioned here, it is always in a negative light.

It is always
>In the USA, wages are better!
>In Europe, houses are cheaper!
>The economy is underperforming!
>My taxes are too high!

There is never any mention of the fantastic benefits which come with life in the best Korea. The fantastic healthcare for minimal cost, the great education system available to everybody, the dynamic modern economy with many bustling sectors, and everything else.

Taxes are higher than the USA but they are lower than most of Europe and we still enjoy fantastic public services whereas in America there are no public services or safety nets.

Not to mention the things you cannot measure. Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs. Enjoying the fantastic beautiful nature and gardens. Living in the historical majesty and balancing that with modern cosmopolitan qualities.

The DRK might not be the best place to become rich but nobody is poor here and it is very easy to enjoy life if you have goals beyond the number in your bank account.
